title: Source-to-Package Release, Incident Command, and Production Defense · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed incident review reconstructs source-to-package release, incident command, and production defense from source and emitted revision, tenant, URL, validator, bytes, runtime validation, Promise settlement, transaction, lease, response, failure, handle cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for defensible production TypeScript feed aggregator after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, cau…
objectives: Bind reviewed source, compiler surfaces, generated artifacts, migrations, lockfile, package contents, emitted JavaScript, image digest, SBOM, signature, configuration, and deployment.
Demonstrate unfamiliar RSS and Atom sources through authorized discovery, bounded Fetch and XML, runtime normalization, transaction, scheduling, API, accessible reader, and cleanup.
Triage publisher overload, stale ingestion, SSRF, entity, sanitizer, search, pool, tenant, async-handle, rollout, and restore incidents with evidence-preserving containment.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Separate evidence from confidence · Review Feed Ts Residual Risk Re…
steps.instruction:
review case — A review TypeScript team handles case 7478 during a cross-tenant privacy and cache audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to defend source-to-package provenance, unfamiliar end-to-end behavior, incidents, recovery, and residual risks; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant work must preserve the prior admitted state; then change one emitted artifact, package, container, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Own unsupported feed constructs, runtime limits, false positives, accessibility findings, capacity boundaries, package risks, deadlines, and stop conditions.
